data.title: American Water Q1 revenue rises on the back of new rates, acquisitions
data.description:
Overview

  • US water utility's Q1 revenue rose yr/yr, supported by new rates and acquisitions

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 declined slightly from prior year

  • Company raised quarterly dividend by 8.2%

Outlook

  • American Water affirms 2026 adjusted EPS guidance range of $6.02 to $6.12

  • Company maintains long-term EPS and dividend growth targets of 7-9%

Result Drivers

  • RATE INCREASES AND ACQUISITIONS - Co said higher operating revenues were driven by new rates from completed general rate cases, infrastructure proceedings, and closed acquisitions

  • HIGHER OPERATING COSTS - Co said operating expenses rose due to increased production costs, including purchased water, power, and chemicals

  • INCREASED DEPRECIATION AND INTEREST - Co said higher depreciation and interest expenses resulted from increased capital investment and additional debt

data.title: Crescent Biopharma Q1 net loss widens on R&D expenses
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. oncology biotech's Q1 net loss and operating loss beat analyst expectations

  • Q1 revenue was $1 mln, up from no revenue a year ago, due to a licensing payment

  • R&D and G&A expenses rose, driven by pipeline advancement and public company costs

Outlook

  • Company expects proof-of-concept data from CR-001 ASCEND trial in Q1 2027

  • Initial data from CR-001 in combination with chemotherapy expected by mid-2027

  • CR-002 Phase 1/2 trial expected to start in H2 2026, with proof-of-concept data in H2 2027

Result Drivers

  • LICENSING REVENUE - Q1 revenue driven by $20 mln upfront payment from Kelun-Biotech license agreement for CR-001, recognized as $1 mln in the quarter

  • PIPELINE DEVELOPMENT - Higher R&D expenses attributed to continued development of CR-001 and CR-002

  • PUBLIC COMPANY COSTS - G&A expenses rose due to personnel costs and professional services tied to public company operations

data.title: CVR Energy Q1 sales beat estimates, adjusted loss widens
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. refiner and fertilizer maker's Q1 sales beat analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 missed analyst expectations

  • Company posted net loss due to $158 mln in unrealized derivative losses

Outlook

  • Company expects Q2 2026 petroleum throughput of 200,000-215,000 bpd

  • Company sees Q2 2026 crude utilization between 92% and 99%

  • Company expects Q2 2026 nitrogen fertilizer ammonia utilization rate of 95%-100%

Result Drivers

  • DERIVATIVE LOSSES - Q1 net loss included $158 mln in unrealized derivative losses, which do not reflect $447 mln in expected value from NYMEX crack spread swaps to be realized through 2027

  • STRONG FERTILIZER DEMAND - Nitrogen Fertilizer segment saw higher net income, supported by robust demand for the spring planting season and higher ammonia and UAN pricing

  • HIGH UTILIZATION RATES - Company reported crude utilization of 97% and ammonia plant utilization of 103%, indicating solid operational performance

data.title: Alphabet Gets Lone Post Earnings Bump Among 'Mag 7' Cos Reporting — Market Talk
data.description: With four of the "Magnificent 7" reporting earnings after the bell, Alphabet alone seems to be doing enough to please investors. Shares of Google's parent company rise 3.9% after results beat expectations, with CEO Sundar Pichai unsurprisingly crediting AI investments underpinning much of the growth across the company. Amazon.com, Meta Platforms and Microsoft all also post robust growth, but shares are down out of the gate, with Meta down more than 5% The Facebook owner forecast sales for its current quarter that's in-line with expectation and also bumped up its capex plans for the year, reflecting higher prices for components and, to a lesser extent, additional data center costs. (paul.ziobro@wsj.com)

data.title: Ethan Allen Q3 revenue falls 5% on weak demand for home furnishings
data.description:
Overview

  • US furniture retailer's fiscal Q3 revenue fell 5% yr/yr amid sluggish home furnishings demand

  • Adjusted EPS for fiscal Q3 declined 37% from prior year

Outlook

data.title: White House ensures its own Fed nightmare
data.description:
By Gabriel Rubin

President Trump’s push to install a pliant central bank majority is backfiring. Outgoing Chair Jay Powell now says he will remain as a member while threats to the institution remain. Add record discord over policy and an oncoming oil shock, and rate cuts are moving out of reach.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Gabriel Rubin on Bluesky and LinkedIn.

CONTEXT NEWS

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said on April 29 that he would stay on the central bank’s board as a governor indefinitely following the conclusion of his term as chair on May 15, at least until he has deemed the threat of a criminal investigation into the Fed’s headquarters renovations to be “truly” over.

Earlier on the same day, the Senate Banking Committee advanced the nomination of Kevin Warsh to be the Fed’s next chair on a party-line 13-11 vote, clearing the way for his confirmation by the full Senate in the coming days.

  • U.S. healthcare communications firm's Q1 revenue fell 8.5% yr/yr

Outlook

  • Company sees 2026 adjusted EBITDA in the range of $27.5 mln to $32.5 mln

Result Drivers

  • MANAGED SERVICES GROWTH - Nearly 57% yr/yr increase in software managed services revenue helped offset declines in other software categories

  • RECURRING REVENUE BASE - Co said over 80% of revenues come from recurring streams, helping mitigate timing and seasonality impacts
